event: The Prophet Joel on the Sistine Chapel ceiling
symbols: scroll being read — the prophetic word of God that Joel received and proclaimed
massive seated figure — the grandeur and authority of the prophetic office
architectural throne — the prophet's exalted role as mouthpiece of God
ignudi (nude figures) flanking — the ideal human form framing the prophetic revelation
description: A fresco panel depicting the prophet Joel seated on an architectural throne, reading intently from a scroll held in both hands. He is an elderly, bald man wearing voluminous robes in orange-red and green-blue tones. His expression is one of intense concentration. The name 'IOEL' is inscribed on a plaque below. Two nude male figures (ignudi) sit on the architectural framework flanking the prophet above. The composition is framed by painted architectural elements including cornices and pilasters.
theological_description: The Prophet Joel occupies a significant place on the Sistine Chapel ceiling as one of the Old Testament prophets whose words prefigure the New Covenant. Joel's most famous prophecy concerns the outpouring of God's Spirit: 'I will pour out my Spirit on all flesh; your sons and your daughters shall prophesy, your old men shall dream dreams, and your young men shall see visions' (Joel 2:28). Peter explicitly identified the fulfillment of this prophecy at Pentecost (Acts 2:16-21), making Joel a bridge between the Old and New Testaments. Joel also proclaimed the Day of the Lord — a day of judgment and restoration — calling Israel to repentance: 'Rend your hearts and not your garments' (Joel 2:13), a verse that became foundational for Christian penitential theology. Michelangelo's depiction of Joel absorbed in reading his own scroll suggests the prophet contemplating the divine mysteries he has been given to proclaim, mysteries whose fulfillment he himself cannot yet see.

scripture_refs.reason: Joel's prophecy of the outpouring of the Spirit on all flesh, the defining prophecy of this prophet
Peter's Pentecost sermon identifying Joel's prophecy as fulfilled in the descent of the Holy Spirit
The call to repentance — rend your hearts and not your garments — Joel's message of inner conversion
The Valley of Decision on the Day of the Lord — Joel's eschatological vision of final judgment

_meta.title: Sistine Chapel Ceiling: The Prophet Joel
